title: What people get when they clone
question: When someone gets this repo to use the tool, should they inherit the author's full decision log, or a small set of examples?
rationale: Cloning the repo handed people the author's own 67 decisions — so it read as a finished project, not a starting point they could make their own. Shipping three small examples instead (one decided, one decided-but-not-built, one open) shows the format and every state a reader needs to recognise, without making anyone gut a log first. The author's real log isn't lost: it moves to its own 'live-log' branch, which is what the public demo serves — so the showcase stays rich while the default branch is a clean template.

options.label: Ship the full real log (what it was doing)

tradeoffs.note: newcomers must delete 67 of someone else's decisions before their own
a real, deep log to learn from
the demo is just this folder

options.label: Ship a few examples; real log on its own branch

tradeoffs.note: three samples to read, then edit or delete
examples are small, but the full log is one branch away
demo serves the live-log branch, which keeps the full log
nothing to delete, but also nothing to copy
no format or states to learn from at all
